jammy-soft-boiled-eggs.name: Jammy Soft-boiled Eggs
instructions: Bring a large saucepan of water to a boil over medium-high heat.
Using a slotted spoon, carefully lower 4–8 large eggs into water one at a time.
Cook 6½ minutes, adjusting heat to maintain a gentle boil.
Transfer cooked eggs to a bowl of ice water and chill until just slightly warm, about 2 minutes.
Gently crack eggs all over and peel, starting from the wider end, which contains the air pocket.
Rinse in cold water to remove any stubborn bits of shell.

jerimus-dumplings-teleculinaria.name: Jerimu's Dumplings

ingredients.item: Sugar syrup or mixture of sugar and cinnamon

instructions: Cut the pumpkin into pieces and bake them in water with a pinch of salt. Then pour the pumpkin, pass it through a pusher and squeeze the mashed potatoes to remove all the water.

Put the pumpkin puree in a bowl, add the flour, the yeast, the yolks, the sugar, the lemon scrape and the port wine and mix it all together nicely. Hit the clears in castle and wrap them in the previous mix.

Bring to the fire a pot with abundant oil. Add spoonfuls of the previous dough and let fry. Remove and let it run on absorbent paper. Serve the cakes watered with a sugar syrup or sprinkled with sugar and cinnamon.

jumbo-shells-stuffed-with-spinach-and-co.name: Jumbo Shells Stuffed With Spinach And Corn
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta shells in the boiling water, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 9 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat grapeseed oil in a deep skillet over medium heat. Add spinach, corn, chicken bouillon, salt, and pepper. Cook until spinach and corn are tender, about 5 minutes.
Drain pasta shells. Fill with corn-spinach mixture. Place shells back into the same deep skillet and cover with diced tomatoes. Mix tomato paste and warm water together and pour onto shells.
Cover and cook on medium heat until tomatoes are heated through, about 10 minutes. Garnish with dried cilantro.
